title: Calculus I
html:
Yahir's MTH 251 Calculus I sequence at OSU in spring 2025 — limits, continuity, the limit definition of the derivative, tangent lines, first/second-derivative shape reading. Recurring source of homework screenshots in the vault. The math-coursework anchor for the same semester that produced the reverse engineering / malware analysis capstone work.

TL;DR

  • 10 inbound citations — concentrated in a tight April 1 → April 22 2025 window.
  • Topics covered (per screenshots): limits, continuity, derivative-from-limit, tangent lines, first/second-derivative analysis.
  • Surfaces in the vault as Tier-1 micro screenshot summaries — Yahir capturing problem sets and worked solutions, not reflecting on them.
  • Same semester as the security capstone — Yahir was juggling foundational math and capstone-level CS in parallel.
  • Functions as a load-bearing prerequisite for the RL coursework that came in June 2025 — the gradient-and-derivative intuition transfers.

Key facts

  • Pure problem-set capture. Almost every linked summary is a Tier-1 micro — a single screenshot of a homework problem or worked example.
  • April 1, April 4, April 8, April 14, April 15, April 22 — densest dates.
  • Earliest screenshot = first canonical mention. 2025 04 01 screenshot 2025 04 01 at 7 32 20 pm 6786bd63.
  • Limits + continuity early; tangent lines and shape later. The spring 2025 progression follows standard MTH 251 sequencing.

Source summaries

All 10 (this is the full citation set):

  • 2025 04 01 screenshot 2025 04 01 at 7 32 20 pm 6786bd63 — first
